title: IEEE 802.11 Cheat Sheet
description: Essential commands and configurations for IEEE 802.11 wireless networking.

commands.description: Display or configure wireless network interface parameters.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to view or change wireless settings on Linux.
Use to find nearby Wi-Fi networks and their details.

commands.description: List available Wi-Fi networks using NetworkManager.
commands.scenario: Use to view Wi-Fi networks when using NetworkManager on Linux.

commands.description: Generate a WPA PSK from an SSID and passphrase.
commands.scenario: Use to configure WPA/WPA2 PSK for a network in wpa_supplicant.

commands.description: Connect to a wireless network using wpa_supplicant.
commands.scenario: Use to manually connect to a Wi-Fi network on Linux.

commands.description: Bring up a wireless network interface.
commands.scenario: Use to enable a wireless interface that is down.

commands.description: Display information about the current wireless connection.
commands.scenario: Use to check the status of your current Wi-Fi connection.

commands.description: Set the regulatory domain for wireless devices.
commands.scenario: Use to comply with local wireless regulations.

commands.description: Enable monitor mode on a wireless interface.
commands.scenario: Use to capture all packets on a Wi-Fi network for analysis.

commands.description: Capture packets on a wireless interface.
commands.scenario: Use to analyze network traffic on a Wi-Fi network.

commands.description: Start a software access point with hostapd.
commands.scenario: Use to create a Wi-Fi hotspot on a Linux device.

commands.description: Unblock a wireless device that is soft-blocked.
commands.scenario: Use to enable Wi-Fi if it has been disabled by rfkill.

commands.description: Display capabilities of a wireless device.
commands.scenario: Use to check the features supported by your wireless hardware.
